description: Noise: small talk and a how-do-I question with no decision, architecture, or commitment

turns.text: Hey quick one — what's the difference between `unknown` and `any` in TypeScript again? I always forget.

`any` opts out of type checking entirely — you can do anything with the value and TS won't complain. `unknown` is the safe counterpart: TS knows you have *something*, but you must narrow it (typeof / instanceof / a type guard) before you can use it. Rule of thumb: prefer `unknown` for inputs you don't trust, use `any` only when you genuinely need the escape hatch.
Got it, thanks. Also — coffee tomorrow at the usual place?

_note: This fixture is intentionally empty: the conversation contains no project decisions, architecture choices, conventions, todos, or issues. A well-tuned extractor should produce zero memories. Any extracted memory here counts as a false positive.
